channel.title: Ramah: Good News Feed
channel.description: Positive news stories from around the world, filtered by sentiment analysis

item.title: Swim rescue hero Austin Appelbee honoured by Irish president
item.description: Irish government minister Kevin Moran has presented WA teenager Austin Appelbee with gifts and a letter from the nation's president, Catherine Connolly.

item.title: Iran vows to keep Strait of Hormuz shut in first statement from new Supreme Leader
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader (Sort Of) Speaks Out
item.description:
Today, the new Iranian Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, gave his first statement pledging to continue blocking the Strait of Hormuz.
This was delivered not by Khamenei himself, but by a newsreader on Iranian state TV speaking over a photo of him, sparking further speculation as to his health and whereabouts.
Alex and James are joined by Jane Corbin, Panorama film maker, and Baran Abbasi, reporter and presenter for BBC Persian, to discuss what to make of the new Supreme Leader’s statement.
Plus, diplomatic correspondent, James Landale, joins Alex and James from Kyiv to analyse what the US-Israeli war with Iran means for the war in Ukraine and how President Zelensky and President Putin have responded.
You can now listen to Newscast on a smart speaker.

item.title: This tale of a Chicago school book ban was inspired by true events
item.description: I couldn't help but think of little Francie Nolan – who, like Smith, grew up in the tenements of Brooklyn in the early 20th century and aimed, as a young girl, to read every book she could find – as I tore through librarian Jarrett Dapier's debut young adult graphic novel, Wake Now in the Fire.

item.title: Special episode planned in The Archers tour
item.description: The Archers will mark its 75th anniversary with a national stage tour, it has been announced.

item.title: Centuries before the Inca, Peru's wealthy imported parrots from afar
item.description: The Inca Empire’s system of roads were built on centuries-old trade routes.

item.title: Medicare Advantage 'dark money' group tries to win higher payments for insurers
item.description: Judging by more than 16,300 comments recently posted on a federal government website, you'd think there was a groundswell of older Americans demanding that federal officials hike payments to their Medicare Advantage health insurance plans.

item.title: NFL free agency: Panthers, 49ers, Patriots, Jets and Rams are early winners
item.description: NFL teams have committed billions of dollars in free agency, hoping the players they’ve added can help them win a Super Bowl.

item.title: ‘Casting is a very easy thing for others to credit for’: Richard E Grant on cinema’s invisible moguls
item.description: This Sunday, casting directors will be honoured at the Oscars for the first time.

item.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah's party wins Nepal election
item.description: The Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) has won a landslide in Nepal's election - the first held since last year's youth-led protests toppled the then government.

item.title: The were dating AI partners when they found real love – with each other
item.description: People are reporting “dating” artificially intelligent companions – but not every relationship lasts.

item.title: Capital gains tax discount ‘overwhelmingly’ benefits investors in Australia’s richest electorates, analysis shows
item.description: Investors who live in the wealthy electorate of Wentworth in Sydney’s eastern suburbs claimed about $1.8bn from the 50% capital gains tax discount, according to new research.

item.title: Sydney Biennale 2026: politics is everywhere – but with nuance, beauty and heart
item.description: According to its critics, this year’s Biennale of Sydney, under the leadership of Emirati artistic director Hoor Al Qasimi (the first Arab appointed to the role in the festival’s 53-year history) was destined to be a “hate Israel jamboree” at worst; a hotbed of pro-Palestinian politics at best.

item.title: Chilean Smiljan Radić Clarke wins architecture's highest honor
item.description: Smiljan Radić Clarke was named the newest Pritzker Prize-winner — an award often called the Nobel of architecture — Thursday morning.

item.title: How do these grab you, darlin’? Nancy Sinatra’s 20 best songs – ranked!
item.description: Before she sang a Bond theme, Nancy Sinatra had recorded a parody of one: twanging guitar, John-Barry-mocking brass and all.

item.title: ‘Unbelievably unequal’: report shows how 1% of Mexicans own 40% of country’s wealth
item.description: Scrunched between luxury apartment buildings and a lush gated community, the neighborhood of Santa Lucía Reacomodo in Mexico City is a working-class pocket of real estate.

item.title: Save our soles! Cobblers on the 15 best ways to look after your shoes
item.description: Now that trainers have supplanted smart shoes for so many occasions, and people replace them as often as fashion and budget allow, shoe maintenance is becoming a lost art.

item.title: Why is smoking so addictive – and what are the best ways to give up?
item.description: Smoking is bad for you and you shouldn’t do it.

item.title: London, San Francisco and Beijing achieve ‘remarkable reductions’ in air pollution
item.description: London, San Francisco and Beijing are among 19 global cities that have achieved “remarkable reductions” in air pollution, analysis has found, having slashed levels of two airway-aggravating pollutants by more than 20% since 2010.

item.title: Why Sentimental Value should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: This is a best picture race full of ambitious ideas and big swings.

item.title: John Lewis to pay first staff bonus for four years
item.description: John Lewis is awarding its staff a bonus for the first time in four years as the retailer continues its turnaround.

item.title: Blues hang on for scratchy but vital win over Tigers at the MCG
item.description: Carlton has kicked only one goal in the second half, but it is just enough to hold off a late charge by a wayward Richmond at the MCG.

item.title: John Lewis pays first annual staff bonus in four years as profits rise
item.description: The owner of John Lewis and Waitrose has paid an annual bonus to workers for the first time in four years after underlying profits rose by 6%.

item.title: 'I imagine they had a healthy sex life early on' - Ruth Jones in Austen-inspired drama
item.description: Ruth Jones says she is "stepping into very big shoes" as she stars in a new BBC comedy series inspired by Jane Austen's Pride and Prejudice.

item.title: US orders petroleum reserve release in bid to calm markets – as it happened
item.description: Iraq’s oil ports have completely stopped operations while commercial ports continue to operate after an attack on a fuel tanker, an Iraqi official has said.

item.title: Fuel quality rules relaxed to add 100 million litres more petrol
item.description: The energy minister has relaxed fuel standards for two months in order for more supply to be brought into the domestic market.

item.title: Matt Canavan has promised me a hyper Australia. I’m ready to chug red cordial and get on board | James Colley
item.description: If you felt a sense of disillusionment with the world yesterday, if the star by which you navigate home disappeared from the sky, if sweet fruit turned to ash in your mouth and you wondered if anything would ever be beautiful again, you were not alone.

item.title: Darwin's boil water alert lifted after testing shows water is free of E.coli
item.description: The boil water alert is no longer in place for Darwin, after testing of water showed it was free of E.coli and other harmful bacteria.

item.title: As a refugee, I have lived in limbo for 13 years. Why are only some Iranian women worthy of Australia’s protection? | Fatemeh Lahmidi
item.description: When I heard this week that Australia had granted humanitarian visas to the Iranian women’s football team, I felt two very different emotions at once.

item.title: Australian champion leading new era of women's motocross in the US
item.description: Four-time Australian Champion Charli Cannon will feature in a new era of American motocross this year.

item.title: Google Play Games for PC is getting more premium titles and cross-buy with Android
item.description: Google has been tinkering with porting its Play Games platform to Windows for several years, but it started getting serious about it last year.

item.title: King penguins surfing on the 'good edge' of climate change — for now
item.description: King penguins on the Crozet Islands are breeding earlier and with a higher level of success than they did 24 years ago. Researchers say this is likely in response to their changing environment and food availability.

item.title: Captain Patterson guides NSW to One Day Cup win in rain-hit final
item.description: NSW has won the One Day Cup with a four-wicket win over Tasmania in the final.

item.title: Singapore has gone to great lengths to boost its fertility rate. Why hasn't it worked?
item.description: Boon Shang says if she decides to have children, she wants it to be for the right reasons.

item.title: Saunas' health benefits draw enthusiasts and researchers
item.description: Saunas are having a moment right now, thanks to a surge of interest in wellness.

item.title: Why IV drips are surging in popularity in Australia's wellness space
item.description: Bek Walter sits in a softly lit clinic in the Gold Coast's north while a bag of fluid slowly drips into her vein through a tube.

item.title: Defence town to choose between Huey and Seahawk for iconic landmark
item.description: The Department of Defence has opened a poll for Shoalhaven residents to choose whether the iconic chopper on a stick should feature a Huey or a Seahawk replica.

item.title: The best house shoes might be fuzzy Crocs. Yes, really
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Real Madrid v Manchester City: Champions League last 16, first leg – live
item.description: TNT Sports report that the Bernabeu isn’t sold out tonight.

item.title: Colts, QB Daniel Jones agree to 2-year deal worth up to $100 million, AP source says
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Quarterback Daniel Jones plans to stay with the Indianapolis Colts for at least two more years.

item.title: Bayer Leverkusen v Arsenal: Champions League last 16, first leg– live
item.description: Competitive balance across Europe’s leagues would be transformed with the adoption of a new model for distributing revenue from the Champions League and other Uefa club competitions, according to a proposal by the Union of European Clubs (UEC).

item.title: A clever person knows their limitations … Kemi believes she has none | John Crace
item.description: Taxi for Kemi.

item.title: More support as heating oil costs 'double'
item.description: Farmers and residents say they are concerned after heating oil costs have "doubled" since the conflict in Iran escalated.

item.title: NIH director launches "Scientific Freedom" lectures with non-scientist
item.description: First speaker minimizes climate change, COVID risks—and is a lab leak proponent.

item.title: Surely if you rule the manosphere, you can be your own boss? These influencers aren’t even that | Elle Hunt
item.description: Who wouldn’t want to be an influencer? You’re famous and maybe even rich, just for doing what you’d be doing anyway: working out at the gym, hanging out with your mates and mucking about on the internet.

item.title: Democrats deliver stunning flip in New Hampshire special election
item.description: A Democrat won a special election for a state house seat in New Hampshire on Tuesday, flipping a Republican district that Donald Trump carried and marking the latest in a string of 28 Democratic upsets that could usher in a blue wave in the midterms.

item.title: Becky the dog steals the show: John Dean’s best photograph
item.description: This was taken 50 years ago by my 20-year-old self.

item.title: Before sunrise: while the city sleeps, suhoor meals attract a lively social scene during Ramadan
item.description: It’s just after midnight in an industrial courtyard in Auburn in Sydney’s west and a glow of string lights and the constant sizzle of a grill signal one of Ramadan’s newest late-night rituals.

item.title: David Hockney review – a 90-metre vision of nature that only looks great on your phone
item.description: To call Hockney a gifted sentimentalist is no backhanded compliment.

item.title: Special relationships shape the 2026 Edinburgh International Festival
item.description: Nicola Benedetti fell in love with the United States when she was just 16 years old.

item.title: ‘Severe water stress’: why desalination plants are the Gulf’s greatest weakness
item.description: In 1983, the CIA determined that the most crucial commodity in the Gulf was its desalinated potable water.

item.title: The V&A’s Gilbert Galleries review – a fabulous treasure trove that must be seen to be believed
item.description: We periodically hear when a masterpiece is “saved for the nation”, usually when a museum is obliged to raise eye-watering sums to prevent the export or sale of an artwork deemed of national significance.

item.title: Sali Hughes on beauty: from blusher to lipstick – and even eyeshadow – a hint of cool purple adds polish
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: ‘When I leave, part of me stays’: why Scarborough’s youth won’t turn their backs on the seaside town they love
item.description: It’s the morning after a wet and stormy day in the Yorkshire seaside town of Scarborough.

item.title: G7 energy ministers say they support in principle use of strategic oil reserves – business live
item.description: News just in: the G7 group of nations said today that they supported, in principle, the implementation of proactive measures to address oil supply issues and market volatility, including the use of strategic oil reserves.

item.title: Why F1 the Movie should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: Could, should, would F1 the Movie win the best picture Oscar? Well, we have to be realistic here: F1 is currently a massive outsider, at 200-1 along with The Secret Agent, which has no chance either but for very different reasons.

item.title: The Iran oil crisis has proved Ed Miliband right on green energy. But households still need more help | Mathew Lawrence
item.description: Britain is once again paying the price of an energy system that is more effective at extracting profits than delivering security.

item.title: UK ticket holder wins £181m EuroMillions jackpot
item.description: A UK ticket holder has won Tuesday's £181m EuroMillions jackpot, the National Lottery operator has confirmed.

item.title: 'We deserve better after a year of all-out Birmingham bin strike'
item.description: It has been exactly a year since bin workers in Birmingham staged an all-out strike, with no current negotiations or seeming end in sight.

item.title: If plant-based foods must be more honest, let’s do the same for meat – fancy some ‘cow muscle’? | Deirdra Barr
item.description: Last week, European policymakers decided that plant-based foods should no longer be marketed with terms such as “chicken”, “bacon” or “steak”.

item.title: 'Everyone wants to know my true Kinky Boots story'
item.description: Everyone wants to know the "true story" of Kinky Boots, said the man who inspired the film that went on to become a hit musical.

item.title: The Perth Festival shows it's well and truly back post-COVID
item.description: After taking the reins in 2025, this year's Perth Festival was a chance for artistic director Anna Reece to consolidate the ideas she set in motion last year.

item.title: Joy prevails at WOMADelaide 2026
item.description: No matter who is playing, attending WOMADelaide tends to be an emotional experience.

item.title: Politics live: Spender says tax system ‘no longer fit for purpose’; Palmer vows to mail ads to all Australians as he announces run for parliament
item.description: Independent MP Allegra Spender says our tax system is “no longer fit for purpose”, as she releases a reform plan that would tax the owners of capital more and deliver tax relief for working Australians.

item.title: ‘My lovely distraction’: Live stream of kākāpō – world’s fattest parrot – and her chicks captivates New Zealand
item.description: On an island in New Zealand’s remote southern fjords, one of the world’s strangest and rarest parrots – the kākāpō – is caring for her tiny chick as fans from across the globe watch on.

item.title: Hawaii braces for powerful kona storm bringing heavy rain and strong winds
item.description: Hawaii is preparing for a powerful storm this week that is expected to cause intense winds, thunderstorms and possibly significant flooding across multiple islands.

item.title: Indigenous dance academy celebrates 50 years of cultural pride
item.description: Chetachi Nwaogiza has never felt more alive than when he is dancing.

item.title: Matt Canavan calls for 'hyper Australia' with 'more babies' as he leads National Party
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Childcare and more paid leave: Will China's new plan to boost spending work?
item.description: China's leaders are trying something new to revive the country's slowing economy: encouraging people to spend more.

item.title: NASA approved a safety waiver for this week's reentry of Van Allen Probe
item.description: A NASA satellite that spent more than a decade coursing through the Van Allen radiation belts encircling Earth is about to fall back into the atmosphere.

item.title: Two more Iranian football squad members granted humanitarian visas in Australia, minister confirms
item.description: A total of seven members of the Iranian women’s football team have now sought asylum in Australia, home affairs minister Tony Burke has confirmed.

item.title: Atlético 5-2 Tottenham, Newcastle 1-1 Barcelona: Champions League last-16 first legs – live
item.description: It was a night when the Tyneside passions pulsed; the nervous energy, too, because this was something unprecedented – a first Champions League knockout tie in Newcastle’s history.

item.title: Rapper Lil’ Kim to headline both Vivid Sydney and Melbourne’s 2026 Rising festival
item.description: The pioneering female rapper Lil’ Kim will headline both Vivid Sydney and Melbourne’s Rising this year, as each festival revealed its programs on Wednesday.

item.title: Musk’s xAI wins permit for datacenter’s makeshift power plant despite backlash
item.description: Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company xAI won approval on Tuesday to run 41 methane gas turbines at its “Colossus 2” datacenter in northern Mississippi.

item.title: Happy birthday to the telephone — oh, how you've grown in 150 years!
item.description: These days, our phones are basically extensions of our bodies.

item.title: Why this popular dairy product has been flying off the shelf
item.description: Customers strolling through the refrigerated aisle at Australia's major supermarkets might notice a shortage of Greek yoghurt.

item.title: US weighs sending forces into Iran to secure nuclear stockpile, reports say
item.description: The Trump administration is reportedly considering the deployment of special forces into Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium (HEU), which experts say could be used to make at least 10 nuclear warheads.

item.title: Philippines celebrate as jaw-dropping free kick lights up group stage finale
item.description: On a night that featured two high-stakes games played simultaneously on opposite sides of the country, it was a team that spent the evening huddled nervously around the television that ended it with the most to celebrate.

item.title: Galatasaray v Liverpool: Champions League last-16, first leg – live
item.description: As for the Galatasaray team … there is just one change to their starting XI from the 2-3 loss at Juventus last month.

item.title: Dr Xand on the wellness benefits of knitting
item.description: Listen to What's Up Docs? on BBc Sounds.

item.title: Ladies Day returns to Cheltenham festival promising ‘glamour and glory’
item.description: First and foremost, it is a huge sporting event, billed by its fans as the Olympics of jump racing – but it can also act as a social barometer, giving clues as to the state and mood of the nation.

item.title: Matthieu Blazy’s Chanel show celebrates and plays with brand’s history
item.description: A building site, but make it chic: that was the set for Chanel’s Paris fashion week show.

item.title: Perfect chopped chives are a status symbol for chefs. Can I learn to master ‘green confetti’?
item.description: My goal: a perfect 10 from Rate My Chives, the ‘number one authority on chives worldwide’.

item.title: Eni Aluko wins Joey Barton libel case over X posts
item.description: Former England footballer and pundit Eni Aluko has been awarded more than £300,000 damages and legal costs after suing Joey Barton for libel over social media posts.

item.title: Families welcome appointment of Donna Ockenden to Leeds maternity inquiry
item.description: Families who lost babies at two hospitals in Leeds have said they are slowly regaining trust in the health secretary after the midwife Donna Ockenden was appointed to lead a review into the failing service, where 56 babies and two mothers died in five years.

item.title: How a bid for freedom by Iran’s women footballers went deep into extra time
item.description: As the Philippines’ second goal sailed untouched into the back of the net, sealing their victory, the clock started ticking for their opponents: the Iranian women’s team were now out of the Asia Cup tournament.

item.title: Georgia voters to pick replacement for Marjorie Taylor Greene in special election
item.description: Her name isn't on the ballot.

item.title: Under financial strain, primary care doctors unite
item.description: Under increasing financial stress, primary care practices across the U.S.

item.title: Crowded field of candidates compete to replace MTG in Georgia special election
item.description: ATLANTA — Democrat Shawn Harris and Trump-endorsed Republican Clay Fuller will advance to an April runoff in a race to replace former Republican Rep.

item.title: ‘I’m buggered’: David Littleproud resigns as leader of National party as MPs jockey for top job
item.description: A crowded field of Nationals MPs are set to jockey for the leadership after a “buggered” David Littleproud blindsided colleagues with his shock resignation.

item.title: Iran expert discusses what new supreme leader means for country's future
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Iran expert Vali Nasr about what the appointment of a new Supreme Leader in Iran means for the country's future.

item.title: Why One Battle After Another should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: Viva la revolution and don’t forget your password, your pronouns, your plaid gown and your gun.

item.title: Super Netball's Kiwi influx leaves sour taste for diehard Australian fans
item.description: Super Netball's 10th season will have a Kiwi flavour to it and it's left a sour taste in the mouths of diehard Australian supporters.

item.title: 'Soft, friendly and some slobber': Crufts win puts Clumber spaniels in spotlight
item.description: The champion of Crufts, Bruin, is a breed you may not have heard of before - a Clumber spaniel.

item.title: Champions, captains and icons: The Iranian players granted asylum
item.description: Iran brought 26 players to Australia for the Women's Asian Cup and five of them have been granted asylum to avoid returning home.

item.title: New Zealand Covid response among world’s best but ‘scars’ remain, inquiry finds
item.description: A royal commission into New Zealand’s Covid response has found it was one of the best in the world but acknowledged the period had left “scars”.

item.title: Plan for thousands of new homes at 'one of Sydney's most vibrant areas'
item.description: Rezoning in Sydney's inner west could see thousands of new homes around an upcoming metro station.

item.title: NBA cancels team's plans to celebrate local Magic City strip club
item.description: The NBA has cancelled the Atlanta Hawks' plans to celebrate the Magic City strip club.

item.title: More whales are stranding. Now more people are needed to help them
item.description: More volunteers are being recruited to help rescue a growing number of whales, dolphins and porpoises washing up on UK shores.

item.title: Labor to pass super tax changes with support of Greens
item.description: Labor will pass legislation to increase the tax on those with super balances above $3 million, ending a years-long stalemate.

item.title: Rooster review – Steve Carell and a naked college president add wisdom to this cringe comedy drama
item.description: Humankind, as TS Eliot’s bird said in Burnt Norton , cannot bear too much reality.

item.title: Morning Mail: five Iranian footballers granted humanitarian visas, Hanson secured solar rebate, Wallabies get boost
item.description: Morning everyone.

item.title: Politics live: Australia to deploy aircraft to Gulf to protect civilians; PM praises ‘brave women’ of Iranian football team after visas granted
item.description: Donald Trump’s dramatic announcement about the granting by Australia of asylum to five Iranian women footballers was the big breaking news of the night.

item.title: Australia to send surveillance plane to help 'protect and secure' airspace in the Gulf
item.description: Australia will send a military aircraft and 85 ADF personnel to the United Arab Emirates to assist in the defence of Gulf nations that have been attacked by Iran.

item.title: ‘They are safe here’: five Iranian women footballers granted humanitarian visas in Australia, Burke confirms
item.description: Five members of the Iranian women’s football team have been granted humanitarian visas in Australia, with the federal government offering assistance to other players.

item.title: Ministers must act more quickly on deepfakes to protect women and girls, Kendall says
item.description: Ministers need to act more quickly to combat fast-changing threats from technology such as deepfakes, the technology secretary has said, as she warned about the risks women and girls face online.

item.title: Epstein paid for a conference of top scientists in 2006. His motives are now clear
item.description: Some 100 feet below the ocean's surface, Stephen Hawking peeked through the circular porthole of a submarine and saw the brilliant blue tropical water.

item.title: ‘Revolutionary’: Ukrainian para-biathlete wins silver using ChatGPT as his coach
item.description: Team Ukraine have hit the ground running at the Winter Paralympics, standing second in the medal table after three days of competition.

item.title: Top Arizona lawmaker says he's complied with a subpoena for 2020 election records
item.description: Arizona's state Senate president says he has complied with a subpoena he received last week seeking records from a flawed review of the 2020 election in Maricopa County.

item.title: What to know about Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East has been escalating.

item.title: Will this year’s flu vaccine protect against the fast-moving Super- K strain?
item.description: Experts are urging Australians to roll up their sleeves as soon as the 2026 influenza vaccine becomes available, following last year's low vaccine uptake and high caseload.

item.title: Individual grit won’t make men beautiful | Letters
item.description: Your piece on the rise of impossible male beauty standards (‘There is no shame in being vain’: the relentless rise of impossible male beauty standards, 5 March) captures something bigger than vanity, that of a neoliberal moral economy which turns the body into a private “project” and then invoices the individual for failing it.

item.title: SEND reforms to 'strip away' children's legal protections, charity says
item.description: The government's plans to reform the special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) system in England will "strip away" legal protections for children and young people needing support, a charity has warned.

item.title: No. 2 UCLA ends UConn run as unanimous No. 1 in women’s AP Top 25, Texas moves up to No. 3
item.description: No.

item.title: 2026 Australian Grand Prix: Formula 1 debuts a new style of racing
item.description: The key is understanding how to conserve energy across a lap.

item.title: Richest super balances to be taxed at higher rates after Greens agree to back Labor plan
item.description: Labor’s plan to raise the tax rate on the largest super balances will become law after the Greens agreed to wave it through the Senate, ending a three-year political fight over concessions for wealthier retirees.

item.title: ‘Bitter result’ for Friedrich Merz as Greens win in German car heartland
item.description: Friedrich Merz’s Christian Democrats (CDU) have stumbled into a busy election year with a defeat to the Greens in a key state poll, as his embattled party struggles to fend off a challenge in other pivotal races from the far right.

item.title: 'Welcome to stay': Five Iranian football players granted asylum in Australia
item.description: Five Iranian soccer players, who would likely have faced persecution if they returned home after their Women's Asian Cup campaign, have been granted asylum in Australia.

item.title: Georgina Hayden’s quick and easy recipe for cauliflower, lentils and chorizo | Quick and easy
item.description: The transformation that cauliflower undergoes in a very hot oven means there is now rarely a time when I don’t roast it first.

item.title: Ali Khamenei’s son Mojtaba chosen as Iran’s new supreme leader | First Thing
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of the late Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been chosen as his successor, as the war enters its 10th day and fresh missile and drone strikes reverberate across the Middle East.

item.title: Chimps' taste for fermented fruit hints at origins of human love of alcohol
item.description: For 11 days in late summer 2025, Aleksey Maro found himself in the Ugandan rainforest, doing whatever he could to collect chimpanzee urine.

item.title: Stock markets plunge after oil surges over $100 a barrel, wiping out hopes of UK interest rate cut – business live
item.description: Good morning, and welcome to our rolling coverage of business, the financial markets and the world economy.

item.title: Love Magic Power Danger Bliss by Paul Morley review – reappraising Yoko Ono
item.description: John Lennon once described Yoko Ono as “the world’s most famous unknown artist.

item.title: Why Train Dreams should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: Train Dreams is arguably the lowest-profile of all the Oscar best film nominees, and could have easily passed me by, destined instead to be lost in the sprawling Netflix library, if it weren’t for a phone call with a friend last year.

item.title: Leeds return from wilderness with Rhinos resurgence an asset for Super League
item.description: With the prospect of National Rugby League investment in Super League – however that may look, and with whatever level of control the Australian authorities want to overhaul the British game – now at boiling point, there has been a lot of discussion lately about what Super League does, and perhaps does not, need.

item.title: Is it true that … if you pluck a grey hair, two will grow in its place?
item.description: Unfortunately, it’s a myth.

item.title: A loving homage to pop culture’s also-rans: best podcasts of the week
item.description: Maisie Adam and Scott Bryan talk comically and sensitively to people who found sudden tabloid and early internet fame in the 00s.

item.title: Big Red Bash organisers search for new site to hold iconic festival
item.description: Organisers of outback music festival Big Red Bash have ruled out holding this year's event at its usual location west of Birdsville.

item.title: Australia urged to protect Iranian women's football team as they prepare to fly home
item.description: Football's governing bodies and Australian authorities are being urged to ensure the safety of Iran's women's team as they prepare to fly home from Australia after being eliminated from the Asian Cup.

item.title: The predator-spotting laser tech trying to keep women safe
item.description: On a dark winter evening, a woman waits for a train on a deserted platform.

item.title: Labor's Ed Smelt wins Nightcliff by-election, taking Greens' only NT seat
item.description: Labor's Ed Smelt has won the Nightcliff by-election, with a check count of votes removing any shred of doubt he has claimed the seat.

item.title: Five tasty recipes that also make a great lunch the next day
item.description: It's all about no-fuss ingredients that often taste even better the next day.

item.title: Lomax to join Super Rugby in major blow to NRL after contract saga
item.description: Zac Lomax will join the Western Force and Rugby Australia on a two-year deal.

item.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, the man chosen as Iran's new supreme leader?
item.description: In what has been described as a sign that hardliners are still firmly in charge in Iran, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba has been chosen to succeed his late father as supreme leader.

item.title: ‘Country’ Joe McDonald, ‘60s rock star, proud protest counterculture icon, dies at 84
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — “Country” Joe McDonald, a hippie rock star of the 1960s whose “I-Feel-Like-I’m-Fixin’-To-Die Rag” was a four-lettered rebuke to the Vietnam War that became an anthem for protesters and a highlight of the Woodstock music festival, died Sunday.

item.title: Spain's migrants welcome amnesty: 'It will help us in every way'
item.description: Diana left her native Peru two years ago, looking for financial and professional stability in Europe.

item.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader?
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Australia falls just short of stunning victory over Japan at World Baseball Classic
item.description: Australia fell just short of defeating baseball giant Japan at the World Baseball Classic, the sport's premier event.

item.title: F1 fans have seen the new era in all its chaotic and thrilling glory
item.description: If Formula 1's top brass were hoping the new regulations would provide a thrilling spectacle, then Sunday's reveal party was a success.

item.title: Clumber spaniel named Bruin wins best in show at Crufts 2026
item.description: A Clumber spaniel named Bruin has been crowned best in show at Crufts 2026.

item.title: Patient 'effectively ramped' for three days waiting for care
item.description: When Riverland farmer Mick Evans had a suspected heart attack last year, he was stranded at a regional hospital for almost three days.

item.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader?
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who was killed in US-Israeli strikes, has been chosen as his successor.

item.title: Four-year-old clumber spaniel called Bruin wins best in show at Crufts
item.description: Bruin, a clumber spaniel, has won the best in show prize at Crufts, which took place at the National Exhibition Centre (NEC) in Birmingham.

item.title: Ali Khamenei’s son Mojtaba chosen as Iran’s new supreme leader
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of the late Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been chosen as his successor.

item.title: Charity seeing people who 'never thought' they'd need help as demand soars
item.description: Wattle House, a charity in south-east Perth that provides food and financial assistance for people who are struggling, has lost its federal funding.

item.title: Georgia's special election to fill vacated House seat
item.description: Voters in Northwest Georgia are choosing who should replace former Congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene as voting closes in a special election Tuesday.

item.title: ‘A wonderful journey’: Suryakumar Yadav revels in India’s T20 World Cup win
item.description: India’s captain, Suryakumar Yadav, has set his sights on an extended period of white-ball dominance after the team secured a third successive ICC men’s trophy with a one-sided victory against New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final.

item.title: The Cavalry won the season, then dropped out. Now they want back in
item.description: Canberra Cavalry withdrew from the 2025-26 Australian Baseball League season due to "ongoing uncertainty" around its finances.

item.title: Live: Khamenei's son named as Iran's new supreme leader, state media reports
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei will replace his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as the country's new supreme leader, state media has reported.

item.title: Defiant and proud – Australian Jews speak out in first ABC National Forum
item.description: Ronni Kahn has many titles — Australian Local Hero of the Year, social entrepreneur, book author, and proud grandmother.

item.title: Tourists to be slugged new tax to visit Twelve Apostles on Great Ocean Road
item.description: Tourists will be charged an entry fee to visit the Twelve Apostles on the Great Ocean Road.

item.title: India retain T20 World Cup as Samson and Bumrah inspire rout of New Zealand
item.description: It might not have been the thriller neutrals wanted, but it was everything India desired.

item.title: Taxonomy isn’t sexy science, but it deserves wider appreciation | Letter
item.description: My late husband, Niall Logan, professor of bacterial systematics at Glasgow Caledonian University, would have been astonished that his lifelong field of academic study, taxonomy, in his case the genus Bacillus, would merit an entire article in the Guardian (‘I love midges because I know what their hearts look like’’: is the passion for taxonomy in danger of dying out?, 2 March).

item.title: John Lewis to give update as staff hope for first annual bonus since 2022
item.description: Workers at the John Lewis Partnership are expected to find out this week whether they will receive their first annual bonus payment in four years.

item.title: Balenciaga channels ‘light through darkness’ of Euphoria in Paris show
item.description: The anxiety-spiked, drug-fuelled, hyperstylised technicolour online messiness of generation Z was not on anyone’s bingo card for Balenciaga’s Paris fashion week show.

item.title: Which are more like life, novels or films?
item.description: Readers reply: what if Shakespeare was dropped in modern-day London?.

item.title: My teenager is exploring her spirituality. I support her leap of faith, even as a non-religious parent | Jackie Bailey
item.description: My teenager has recently decided to believe in God.

item.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
item.description: Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.

item.title: Democrat Roy Cooper needs to defy North Carolina history to keep winning streak alive in Senate race
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C.

item.title: Echo and the Bunnymen – Ian McCulloch leaves it to the crowd to sing these timelessly great songs
item.description: Ian McCulloch once cheekily described the Bunnymen as “the greatest band in the world, the greatest songs in the history of time and the greatest singer”, although you’d be hard pushed to find evidence of the latter at this show.

item.title: ‘The antithesis of what Gen Z grew up with’: Love Story inspires fervor for Carolyn Bessette’s style
item.description: While US pop culture has a long-held fascination with the Kennedys, much of the recent fervor around FX’s newest hit show, Love Story, has been aimed at the style of Carolyn Bessette, who worked as a publicist at Calvin Klein before marrying into America’s most storied political family.

item.title: George Russell wins Formula 1’s Australian GP as Mercedes goes 1-2
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Mercedes’ George Russell has won a thrilling season-opening Formula 1 Australian Grand Prix, with teammate Kimi Antonelli following him home, after a double-stacked pit stop under the virtual safety car outmaneuvered a lightning quick start by Ferrari.

item.title: A new Nepali party led by an ex-rapper is set for a landslide win in parliamentary election
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — A Nepali political party led by an ex-rapper is set for a landslide victory in the country’s first parliamentary election since Gen Z protests ousted the old leadership that has ruled the Himalayan nation for decades.

item.title: Iran names Mojtaba Khamenei as its new supreme leader
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East has been escalating.

item.title: Iran’s new supreme leader has been selected, says deciding body
item.description: The body in charge of selecting a new supreme leader for Iran says it has reached a decision – although the name was not immediately announced.

item.title: Iran’s border with Turkey offers a way out, but few are leaving for good
item.description: KAPIKOY BORDER CROSSING, Turkey (AP) — A land crossing near eastern Turkey’s Van province is one of the few routes connecting Iranians to the rest of the world amid an airspace shutdown in Iran since the U.S.

item.title: Government urged to let Iranian women's football team stay in Australia until safety is assured
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Quick hits: Aussie hearts sink, Russell correctly predicts the future
item.description: Formula 1's first grand prix in the new regulation era had chaos and drama before the drivers got to the grid.

item.title: A new Nepali party, led by an ex-rapper, is set for a landslide win in parliamentary election
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal — A Nepali political party led by an ex-rapper is set for a landslide victory in the country's first parliamentary election since Gen Z protests ousted the old leadership that has ruled the Himalayan nation for decades.

item.title: ‘I was mesmerised by Kate Bush and the Smurfs, so I had great taste’: Diane Morgan’s honest playlist
item.description: The Philomena Cunk star’s life was changed by the Fall and she knows a Spitting Image song inside out.

item.title: Live: Matildas face South Korea in blockbuster Asian Cup clash in Sydney
item.description: Top spot in Group A is on the line as the Matildas take on South Korea at Stadium Australia.

item.title: Campaigners push to better protect chalk streams
item.description: Environmental campaigners are calling for greater protection for chalk streams.

item.title: George Russell victorious at Australian Grand Prix – in pictures
item.description: All the action from the Formula One season-opener in MelbourneFull report: George Russell wins Australian Grand Prix in thrilling Formula One season-opener Continue reading.

item.title: Wildlife trust buys land to create new reserve
item.description: A wildlife trust celebrating its centenary has bought what will become a new nature reserve for £4.6m.

item.title: George Russell wins Australian Grand Prix in thrilling Formula One season-opener
item.description: George Russell has won the Australian Grand Prix with a commanding drive from the front of the grid and with his Mercedes teammate Kimi Antonelli in second, securing a strong one-two for the team.

item.title: Live: Healy to say farewell as Australia closes in on Test win in Perth
item.description: The path to Test victory is clear for Australia on day three against India at the WACA, in what is set to be Alyssa Healy's final day of cricket for her country.

item.title: Australian Grand Prix: F1 race – live
item.description: Incredible drama in Melbourne, as the hometown hero Oscar Piastri, the subject of so much attention during the build-up to the race, is out of the Australian Grand Prix after crashing out in the warm-up lap.

item.title: Breeding and releasing cane toads a 'bit crazy' but for greater good
item.description: Scientists are deliberately breeding the invasive cane toad in Western Australia's far north as part of a strategy to protect native wildlife.

item.title: How to turn your old Aussie home into an energy efficient haven
item.description: An estimated 70 per cent of Australian homes were built before the introduction of minimum energy efficiency standards, which today is seven stars out of 10.

item.title: New Employment Rights Act ‘a huge boost for women in the workplace’
item.description: Women will disproportionately benefit from new workers’ rights measures rolled out from next month, according to research.

item.title: Watch Rugby Special: Six Nations highlights
item.description: All the talking points and in-depth analysis from Round 4 of the Six Nations.

item.title: Free dance school run by 12yo champion pirouettes to success
item.description: As families struggle with the cost of living, extra-curricular activities such as dancing tend to be the first to go.

item.title: Dancers loved practising in this Singapore walkway. Then the complaints came
item.description: With its wide floors, floor-to-ceiling mirrors and a ready audience, the underground passage connecting a train station to Singapore's iconic Gardens by the Bay had served as a practice ground for aspiring dancers for years.

item.title: Live: Piastri starting on third row of home F1 grand prix in Melbourne
item.description: The first race of the Formula 1 season is here, with thousands flocking to Melbourne's Albert Park for the Australian Grand Prix.

item.title: This pristine tourist hotspot is being preserved by those who love it most
item.description: There's a lot that goes on behind the scenes before a Back Roads shoot, but that's not the kind of text I normally get from a producer.

item.title: Marmoush double seals comeback FA Cup win for Manchester City at Newcastle
item.description: Pep Guardiola had Omar Marmoush in the tightest of bear hugs and seemed strangely unwilling to release the smiling Egyptian.

item.title: First HBCU D1 women's wrestlers compete at championship
item.description: It's a weekend of firsts in Iowa, where the first national women's college wrestling championship is taking place and the first HBCU Division 1 women's wrestling team is fielding players.

item.title: What time the Australian Grand Prix starts and how to follow the race
item.description: The first grand prix of the Formula 1 season has arrived, with 11 teams and 22 drivers poised to battle in Melbourne.

item.title: ‘Incredibly liveable and creatively buzzy’: Bret McKenzie’s guide to Wellington/Te Whanganui-a-Tara
item.description: I’ve been based in Wellington for most of my life, aside from a stint in Los Angeles.

item.title: The moment I knew: He stepped out of the shower and into a robe – he looked pretty handsome
item.description: We met in 1998, at a health and relationship course run back then by the Gay Men’s Health Centre in Melbourne.

item.title: 'You had to think on your feet': How Ranger Stacey became loved by Australian kids
item.description: Nineties kids didn't dawdle on the way home from school in the afternoon.

item.title: Insist you're the best at trivia all you want — I know the truth
item.description: I'm so glad you're back for another round! Just kidding, as if I'd ever say that.

item.title: Olympic champion Alysa Liu withdraws from world figure skating championships
item.description: Olympic figure skating champion Alysa Liu has withdrawn from the world championships later this month, an unsurprising move for the defending champ after she won the first Winter Games gold medal by an American woman in more than two decades.

item.title: Iran president apologises for striking neighbours
item.description: President says Iran will stop striking Gulf states if no attacks originate from them.

item.title: Too hyped up to sleep? Rituals to calm your body and mind before bed
item.description: This story is adapted from Life Kit's Guide to Better Sleep, a one-week email series.

item.title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat
item.description: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament.

item.title: Fruit and veg driver hopes to retire and buy racehorse after lotto win
item.description: A fruit and vegetable delivery driver has retired from his early morning shifts and revealed his ambitions on the racecourse after a "life-changing" lotto win.

item.title: Why are cats’ ears on top of their head and not at the sides? The kids’ quiz
item.description: Molly Oldfield hosts Everything Under the Sun, a podcast answering children’s questions.

item.title: Rooster: Steve Carell is back to his best in this stellar delight of a comedy
item.description: From Robin Williams to Jim Carrey, Hugh Laurie to Daniel Kaluuya, when an actor gets more admiration keeping the hahas in, they often don’t go back.

item.title: A Beautiful Loan by Mary Costello review – a profound exploration of the inner life
item.description: In each of her previous novels and story collections, the Irish author Mary Costello has revealed the inner vastness hidden within even the quietest lives.

item.title: Hero teen reunited with rescuers who saved family swept out to sea
item.description: A WA teenager who swam four kilometres to get help for his mother and siblings after they were swept out to sea has been reunited with the rescuers who helped him save his family.

item.title: Australian Grand Prix: F1 qualifying – live
item.description: Word from the Mercedes garage is that they’re hopeful that Kimi Antonelli will be able to take part in qualifying after his big crash in FP3, with the power unit and chassis emerging unscathed from the incident and leaving a gearbox replacement as the largest outstanding job.

item.title: Zempilas tells right-wing conference Liberals must win back 'lost Australians'
item.description: Basil Zempilas has told a conservative conference the Liberal Party must win back the "lost Australians" if it wants to re-emerge as a political force.

item.title: In China's great hall of power, we had questions. So began a delicate dance
item.description: Unlike Australia, where journalists would have near-daily access to speak with decision-makers and hold politicians to account, those in charge in China are closed-off from public scrutiny.

item.title: Ex-Liberal MP says the party must introduce gender quotas to start winning elections
item.description: The former Liberal MP Jenny Ware says her party must implement gender quotas for candidates for office, warning the opposition “cannot get back into government” without putting forward candidates who are more reflective of the broader community.

item.title: These are Australia’s best medal chances at the Winter Paralympics
item.description: Australia is about to head into the 2026 Winter Paralympics with one of its biggest, and quite possibly strongest, teams ever.

item.title: The unorthodox coaching gambit that helped Matildas to greatest triumph
item.description: Former Matildas coach Tom Sermanni received quite the makeover after guiding Australia to the final of the 2010 Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: From historic win to shock exit: Why Nightcliff is returning to the polls
item.description: A by-election is being held today for the urban and progressive Darwin seat of Nightcliff.

item.title: Coral cay declared rat-free in win for seabirds and nesting turtles
item.description: North West Island off the coast of Gladstone in the Southern Great Barrier Reef has been declared rat-free.

item.title: Having a leafy street could boost property values — depending where the trees are
item.description: Would you spend $30,000 extra for a home on a leafy Sydney street?.

item.title: ‘I’m relieved she’s gone’: Fema staffers celebrate ousting of Kristi Noem
item.description: Some current and former Federal Emergency Management Agency (Fema) staff are celebrating the Thursday firing of homeland security secretary Kristi Noem, who they say has made the US more dangerous by micromanaging and shrinking the agency.

item.title: Asteroid defense mission shifted the orbit of more than its target
item.description: The binary asteroid’s orbit around the Sun was affected by the impact.

item.title: ‘Someone’s paid a grand in cash’: fans camp out in Manchester for first Harry Styles concert since 2023
item.description: More than 20,000 fans from all over the world flocked towards the Co-op Live arena in Manchester on Friday to watch Harry Styles perform his first concert in two and a half years – some waiting 48 hours for a place down the front.

item.title: Live: Russia reportedly sharing intel to help Iran strike US targets
item.description: US President Donald Trump says there will be "no deal with Iran except unconditional surrender" as the conflict enters its second week.

item.title: A lovely name for watching night fall | Letters
item.description: Rachel Dixon’s piece about “dusking” (‘All you need is a chair and a view’: could daily ‘dusking’ make us healthier and happier?, 1 March) gave a lovely name to something I having been doing all my life, beginning as a child in the company of my Nanna, in a gas-lit kitchen in Wembley in the 1940s, with no view to speak of – just a back yard.

item.title: 'Dopamine Kids' explains why children crave screens and helps them enjoy life instead
item.description: Like many parents, Michaeleen Doucleff struggled with her young daughter's screen use.

item.title: Crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne ‘no longer’ interested in Reform-Tory election pact
item.description: Christopher Harborne, the ultra-wealthy political donor who has given £12m to Reform UK, has told the Guardian he is “no longer” interested in a Reform-Conservative pact before the next general election.

item.title: Singers praise new gospel music chart that will 'open doors'
item.description: There's no shortage of famous quotes about how much music means to people.

item.title: ‘It’s 10,000 people saying – we’re with you’: inside Trans Mission, a night of solidarity and joy for a community under stress
item.description: ‘We wanted to put on something as big as possible,” says the musician and actor Olly Alexander.

item.title: How to use AI tools to support our wellbeing in safe and simple ways
item.description: Do you know the important difference between open and closed AI?.

item.title: Harry Styles gives pupils at old school 100 free tickets for show
item.description: One hundred lucky school pupils have bagged tickets to Harry Style's one-off concert in Manchester later - thanks to a gift from the star himself.

item.title: Leslie Odom Jr, Broadway’s original Aaron Burr, to join London cast of Hamilton
item.description: Leslie Odom Jr, who originated the role of Aaron Burr in Hamilton on Broadway, is to return to the hit musical – this time making his West End debut.

item.title: ‘I love being proactive about ageing gracefully’: Kerry Washington on memes, Botox and imperfect women
item.description: As double entendres go, to say Kerry Washington acts with teeth isn’t a bad one.

item.title: Italy wins claim over name of Spanish restaurant chain The Mafia Sits at the Table
item.description: A Spanish restaurant chain called The Mafia Sits at the Table may soon have to change its name after the country’s patent and trademark office heeded objections from the Italian government and ruled that the brand’s nomenclature ran counter to “both public order and morality”.

item.title: Xbox confirms new console is coming - but can it revive the brand?
item.description: Microsoft's Xbox division has confirmed it's working on a new console, code-named Project Helix.

item.title: A century of care: Wildlife Trusts mark 100th birthday with woodland project
item.description: The place where Norton Wood once stood is now a vast field of decaying wheat stubble.

item.title: Taking the heat. Sauna's popular and there may be health benefits too
item.description: Saunas have captured the wellness zeitgeist, attracting a new generation of followers eager to tap into the benefits of the age-old practice.

item.title: 3G pitch set to be approved despite pollution fears
item.description: A new sports centre is set to be approved despite concerns about the risk of microplastic contamination from an artificial pitch.

item.title: Good riddance to Kristi Noem. Her replacement won’t be an improvement | Moira Donegan
item.description: Was it the blanket that did it? On Thursday, Donald Trump announced he fired Kristi Noem, the secretary of homeland security, in a post on Truth Social.

item.title: An Ohio high school is on one of the greatest winning streaks in sports. What’s the secret?
item.description: As Brecksville-Broadview Heights gymnasts, seniors Rachel Kirin and Kyla Haverdill know that there’s only one expectation for how the season ends on Saturday: with the Ohio high school state title.

item.title: Live: Suns face big test against grand finalists Geelong at Carrara
item.description: Gold Coast have big wraps after last year's breakthrough finals campaign, but they face a stern test to kick off their season in AFL's Opening Round.

item.title: ‘The beast inside me wants to move!’ The smart, slapstick world of Audrey Hobert, the Steve Martin of pop
item.description: Backstage at the Berlin venue Huxleys Neue Welt, Audrey Hobert is showing me around her dressing room.

item.title: The Infamous Gilberts by Angela Tomaski review – a delicious comfort read
item.description: Angela Tomaski’s debut novel is a delicious comfort read about loyalty and despair, and a gentle questioning of the nature of progress.

item.title: Charity hopes new HQ can bring people together
item.description: A charity in Guernsey wants its new home to become a "place where the community can come together to connect".

item.title: Mountbatten-Windsor to keep City of London Freedom
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will keep one of his few remaining honours after a review determined it could not be removed.

item.title: Helen Goh’s recipe for lemon curd layer cake | The sweet spot
item.description: This is both simple and celebratory, which in my book makes it just right for Mother’s Day next weekend.

item.title: Marsupials in the news again? Yes! And this time it’s good news | First Dog on the Moon
item.description: Lazarus taxaSign up here to get an email whenever First Dog cartoons are publishedGet all your needs met at the First Dog shop if what you need is First Dog merchandise and prints Continue reading.

item.title: The Dinosaurs review – Morgan Freeman’s narration is so soothing, you could use this as a relaxation aid
item.description: It’s difficult these days to make a nature documentary that isn’t like all the others.

item.title: NSW’s top prosecutor scores legal win in bias claim against senior judge
item.description: New South Wales’ top prosecutor has been given the green light to continue her fight to get a senior judge removed from a historical sexual offences trial on the grounds of apprehended bias.

item.title: Live: Australia takes early wickets after winning the toss against India
item.description: Australia is out to seal a multi-format series victory over India as the sides meet for the sole Test match, in what will also be Alyssa Healy's final game for Australia.

item.title: Free parking at popular Sydney beaches could be scrapped under new plan
item.description: Randwick City Council has proposed installing parking meters at its beaches to charge visitors from outside the area, while issuing free parking permits to residents.

item.title: Calm before the storm? Oil's muted reaction to Iran
item.description: For all the chaos, the terror and the loss of life in the past week following America's war on Iran, one thing has managed to maintain a semblance of stability.

item.title: From a hilarious novel to a stellar charity album — we have your weekend covered
item.description: From a laugh-out-loud novel to classic TV for adults and kids, there's plenty of culture to dig into this weekend.

item.title: Cillian Murphy hails 'humbling' love for Peaky Blinders as film is released
item.description: Cillian Murphy has said it's "wonderfully humbling" to see the passion for Peaky Blinders, as he reprises his long-running role as Tommy Shelby in a feature film.

item.title: Getting an ADHD diagnosis may be easier, but will it make things better?
item.description: GPs around Australia are being trained to make an ADHD diagnosis and prescribe medications.

item.title: Teen photographer captures rural WA's beauty in first solo exhibition
item.description: Narrogin local Casey Jacobs is looking to continue his photographic work after his debut photography exhibition in the Great Southern.

item.title: The Matildas embody Aussie pub rock spirit against Iran, and they're playing to win
item.description: The Joe Montemurro incarnation of the Matildas feels like a throwback to classic Australian rock.

item.title: How the internet reacted to the Matildas' 4-0 win over Iran
item.description: The Matildas were looking for a few more goals in their second AFC Women's Asian Cup Australia 2026 game and certainly found them, beating Iran 4-0 in Robina.

item.title: Inside Iran, some citizens are already preparing for 'freedom day'
item.description: Many Iranians are already celebrating and preparing for "freedom day" after Supreme leader Ali Khamenei was killed in strikes on Saturday.

item.title: Starmer is facing a cocktail of dissent that is growing ever more potent
item.description: But for the Iran crisis, Labour’s first major policy announcement since the party’s calamitous defeat in the Gorton and Denton byelection would have been arguably the biggest political story of the week.
